text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Head and Neck Surgery / Otolaryngology
PROCEDURE: Direct Laryngoscopy with Biopsy, Right Base of Tongue; Fine Needle Aspiration, Right Level II Cervical Lymph Node
CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old female with 3-month history of progressive right-sided odynophagia and palpable right neck mass. Oropharyngoscopy revealed an exophytic, friable mass at the right base of tongue. Evaluation for primary malignancy.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Right base of tongue mass - forceps biopsy (3 fragments in formalin)
  B. Right level II cervical lymph node - fine needle aspiration (direct smears and cell block)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. RIGHT BASE OF TONGUE, BIOPSY:
   - INVASIVE SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 2)
   - p16 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY: STRONGLY AND DIFFUSELY POSITIVE (see ancillary studies)
   - HPV RNA IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION: POSITIVE FOR HIGH-RISK HPV (TYPE 16) (see ancillary studies)
   - See comment.

B. RIGHT LEVEL II CERVICAL LYMPH NODE, FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION:
   - POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS, CONSISTENT WITH METASTATIC SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA
   - p16 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (CELL BLOCK): POSITIVE
   - See comment.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right Base of Tongue Biopsy):
The biopsy fragments demonstrate an infiltrating squamous cell carcinoma arising from the oropharyngeal mucosa of the right base of tongue. The tumor is moderately differentiated (AJCC/CAP grade 2) and exhibits architectural features including irregular nests and sheets of neoplastic squamous cells with central keratinization (keratin pearl formation), intercellular bridge formation (desmosomes), and individual cell dyskeratosis. Tumor cells display enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei with prominent nucleoli, coarse chromatin, and moderate-to-abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures, including atypical forms, are readily identified. There is a desmoplastic stromal reaction with admixed chronic inflammatory infiltrate, including a prominent tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) component at the tumor-stromal interface, which is characteristic of HPV-associated oropharyngeal SCC. No surface epithelial dysplasia is identified in the adjacent mucosa (non-keratinizing surface overlying the invasive carcinoma). Assessment of surgical margins is not applicable in the context of diagnostic biopsy.

The immunohistochemical profile is consistent with squamous cell differentiation: CK5/6 strongly positive, p63 strongly positive in tumor nuclei, CK7 negative. This profile, combined with the morphology, confirms squamous cell lineage.

p16 immunohistochemistry demonstrates block-like, diffuse, strong nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in greater than 75% of tumor cells (score 3+), meeting the College of American Pathologists (CAP) and ISCAP criteria for p16 positivity as a surrogate marker of transcriptionally active high-risk HPV infection in oropharyngeal SCC.

HPV RNA chromogenic in situ hybridization (CISH), performed using a high-risk HPV RNA probe cocktail (targeting HPV types 16, 18, 31, 33, 45, 51, 52, 56, 58, 66), demonstrates punctate and diffuse dot-pattern intranuclear RNA signal in the majority of tumor cells, consistent with transcriptionally active high-risk HPV infection. Genotype-specific follow-up testing confirms HPV type 16 RNA expression.

Ki-67 (MIB-1) proliferation index: approximately 65% of tumor nuclei positive, reflecting the high proliferative activity characteristic of HPV-driven oropharyngeal SCC.

EBER in situ hybridization: NEGATIVE (performed to exclude nasopharyngeal carcinoma with EBV association).

Taken together, the histomorphologic, immunohistochemical, and in situ hybridization findings are diagnostic of HPV-associated (p16-positive, HPV type 16 RNA-positive) invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue, moderately differentiated. This is consistent with the clinically and radiographically identified primary oropharyngeal tumor.

Specimen B (Right Level II Cervical Lymph Node, Fine Needle Aspiration):
Direct smears are highly cellular and demonstrate dispersed and loosely cohesive clusters of atypical epithelial cells with irregular nuclear contours, prominent nucleoli, and moderate cytoplasm, set against a background of lymphocytes and necrotic debris, consistent with metastatic squamous cell carcinoma in a lymph node. Cell block preparations confirm the presence of malignant squamous cells. p16 immunohistochemistry performed on the cell block shows strong, diffuse, block-like nuclear and cytoplasmic positivity in the tumor cells, identical in pattern to the primary biopsy. These findings confirm metastatic involvement of the right level II cervical lymph node by p16-positive squamous cell carcinoma, concordant with the right base of tongue primary.

NOTE: Additional molecular testing (NGS comprehensive panel, PD-L1 CPS by IHC) has been submitted separately and results will be reported as an addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and "right base of tongue biopsy," are three tan-pink soft tissue fragments ranging in greatest dimension from 0.2 cm to 0.5 cm. The combined greatest dimension is 0.5 x 0.3 x 0.2 cm. Cut surfaces are tan-white and firm with focal areas of hemorrhage. All material is submitted entirely in one cassette labeled A1.

Specimen B: Received are four direct smears (two air-dried, two alcohol-fixed) and a cell block prepared from residual FNA material, labeled with patient name and "right level II cervical lymph node FNA." The cell block is tan-pink, measuring 0.4 x 0.3 x 0.2 cm. The cell block is submitted entirely in one cassette labeled B1.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Specimen A - Right Base of Tongue):
  Performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue sections. Appropriate positive and negative controls performed concurrently.

  p16 (CINtec, clone E6H4):         POSITIVE - Block-like, strong, diffuse nuclear and cytoplasmic staining; >75% of tumor cells (3+). Meets CAP criteria for HPV surrogate positivity.
  CK5/6 (D5/16B4):                  POSITIVE - Strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells.
  p63 (4A4):                         POSITIVE - Strong, diffuse nuclear staining in tumor cells.
  CK7 (OV-TL 12/30):                NEGATIVE - No staining in tumor cells (focal staining in entrapped glandular structures only).
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):                     POSITIVE - Approximately 65% of tumor nuclei (high proliferative index).
  EBER ISH:                          NEGATIVE - No nuclear signal in tumor cells.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Specimen B - Right Level II Lymph Node, Cell Block):
  p16 (CINtec, clone E6H4):         POSITIVE - Strong, diffuse, block-like nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in metastatic tumor cells.

IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION:
  HPV RNA ISH (high-risk probe cocktail, HPV types 16/18/31/33/45/51/52/56/58/66):
    Specimen A:                       POSITIVE - Punctate and diffuse intranuclear RNA signal in the majority of viable tumor cells, consistent with transcriptionally active high-risk HPV. Genotype specific confirmation: HPV TYPE 16 positive.

ADDITIONAL STUDIES - PENDING:
  PD-L1 Combined Positive Score (CPS) by immunohistochemistry (22C3 pharmDx, Keytruda companion diagnostic): PENDING - results to follow in addendum.
  Next-generation sequencing (NGS) comprehensive solid tumor panel: submitted to [Molecular Pathology Laboratory], results to be reported as a separate report.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This case represents a 52-year-old never-smoking female with a histologically confirmed diagnosis of HPV-associated (p16 3+ positive, HPV-16 RNA ISH positive) invasive squamous cell carcinoma, moderately differentiated, arising at the right base of tongue. Regional metastasis is confirmed in the right level II cervical lymph node by FNA cytology with concordant p16 positivity. These findings are consistent with clinically and radiographically staged T2N1M0 oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, which by AJCC 8th edition HPV-positive oropharyngeal staging corresponds to pathologic stage I (p16-positive cohort). The absence of TP53 immunohistochemical overexpression (note: formal TP53 IHC not performed; TP53 assessment pending on NGS panel), the presence of prominent tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, the non-keratinizing morphology of the dominant tumor component, and the diffuse block-like p16 positivity are all features highly characteristic of HPV-driven oropharyngeal SCC with a generally favorable prognosis compared to HPV-negative counterparts.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DICTATED BY: [Attending Pathologist, Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine]
TRANSCRIBED AND VERIFIED BY: [Pathology Staff]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGN-OUT: [Signature on file]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Addendum (if applicable): PD-L1 CPS result and NGS panel results will be issued as addenda upon completion.
